seoTitle: Create and Read Error Bars in Power BI Charts
metaDescription: See how to add error bars to Power BI visuals and correctly interpret what they show, so your charts communicate uncertainty instead of hiding it.
intro: Error bars turn a flat chart into an honest one by showing how much variability sits behind each data point. This guide walks through adding error bars to a Power BI visual and, just as importantly, explains how to read them once they're on the screen. It's written for anyone who reports statistical results and wants viewers to trust the numbers.

steps.heading: Understand why error bars matter
steps.body: Before building anything, get clear on what error bars represent — the range of variability or confidence around a measured value — so you don't misuse them in a report.

steps.heading: Create the error bars
steps.body: Add error bars to your chosen visual in Power BI and configure the values that define the upper and lower bounds for each data point.

steps.heading: Interpret the significance
steps.body: Learn to read the length of each bar as a signal of how reliable a data point is; wider bars mean more uncertainty, narrower bars mean tighter confidence.

steps.heading: Apply error bars to a business scenario
steps.body: Use error bars in a real report, such as showing the reliability of forecasted sales figures, so stakeholders understand the range behind the average.

steps.heading: Combine with other Power BI features
steps.body: Pair error bars with tooltips or drill-throughs so viewers can get more context without cluttering the main visual.

faq.question: What do error bars actually measure in Power BI?
faq.answer: They represent the range of variability or uncertainty around a value, typically based on standard deviation, standard error, or a confidence interval you define.

faq.question: Can I add error bars to any chart type in Power BI?
faq.answer: Error bars work best with visuals like bar and column charts where a single point value can be paired with a defined upper and lower range.

faq.question: Why would a business report need error bars?
faq.answer: They help stakeholders avoid over-trusting a single number by showing how much a metric could reasonably vary, which matters for forecasts and small sample sizes.

faq.question: Do error bars slow down report performance?
faq.answer: They add a modest amount of calculation but generally have minimal impact compared to other visual elements in a typical Power BI report.
